educational_objective: Puzzles about strategy — turn piece, compare, try again. Low-frustration; child sees picture first.
age_stage_nuance: Versus 28–30m shape sorters and peg puzzles: reject baby peg puzzles mastered months ago; need 3–12 piece jigsaws with real interlocking pieces and a visible picture to work from.
what_to_look_for: 3–12 pieces; chunky; clear image; progressive sets; UK mainstream (Galt, Orchard Toys, Ravensburger junior, Melissa & Doug).
what_to_avoid: Peg puzzles as Top Picks; 50+ pieces; flimsy cardboard; age-18m marketed as a fresh 34–36m buy.
methodology: Searched UK brand sites, Amazon UK, John Lewis and editorial roundups (Mumsnet, parent blogs). Built a 15-candidate bench of real interlocking jigsaws in the 3–12 piece band, verified piece counts and age marks, then ranked for strategy-fit, age honesty, evidence and gift suitability. Cross-checked against 28–30m peg/shape-sorter picks to avoid baby repeats.

safety_sources_checked: Manufacturer age marks and choking-hazard warnings on product pages
preloved_sources_checked: General Vinted/eBay puzzle category suitability — cardboard sets common pre-loved

key_specs.included_items: Two farm-animal jigsaws; talk guide on box
key_specs.other: Strong colour/background clues on each piece

top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: Two chunky 12-piece farm jigsaws in one box. Wipe-clean cardboard pieces with bold background colours so the picture stays visible while they work.
top_picks.ember_verdict: This is the step up from peg posting: real interlocking pieces, but the pig and cow scenes stay readable while they turn and compare. Strong visual clues mean frustration stays low — good third-birthday gift if peg puzzles are already easy.
top_picks.personalization_hint: likely to be comparing piece shapes and turning them to match colours on the picture
top_picks.why_it_fits: 12 pieces hits the strategy brief without jumping to 20+; UK-made Orchard quality; clear picture-first design; strong Amazon and brand-site proof.
top_picks.caveats: Age mark says 2+ — honest for nearly three, not a fresh 18m buy. Start with one puzzle out; save the second for a win later in the week.
top_picks.gift_note: Reliable small-box gift under £10; wrap-friendly and familiar on UK nursery shelves.
top_picks.ownership_note: Skip if you already own Orchard 12-piece farm or jungle sets from a younger sibling — borrow a different theme first.
top_picks.safety_notes: Not suitable under 3 years per small-parts warning on some Orchard 12-piece lines — supervise if mouthing is still common.
top_picks.review_quality_note: Parents praise chunky pieces and wipe-clean board; some note 12 pieces is ambitious before 30m — right band for 34–36m.
top_picks.preloved_signal_note: Common on Vinted; check all 24 pieces present — Orchard sells replacement pieces.
top_picks.evidence_notes: Mumsnet names Orchard progressive jigsaws among trusted toddler puzzle brands.

key_specs.piece_count: 4 puzzles: 4-piece and 8-piece mix; 6 extra vehicle play pieces
key_specs.included_items: Construction vehicle jigsaws plus character/cone play pieces

top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: Four construction-vehicle jigsaws in one box — mix of four-piece and eight-piece puzzles — plus six extra vehicle play pieces.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Age-honest 3+ mark and a built-in ladder: four-piece diggers first, eight-piece cement mixers when they want more trial-and-error. Vehicle interest keeps them at the table long enough to practise turning pieces.
top_picks.personalization_hint: likely to be naming vehicles and lining up finished puzzles beside toy diggers
top_picks.why_it_fits: Progressive 4→8 pieces inside the brief; honest 3+ age mark; distinct from farm/animal sets; strong Orchard durability reputation.
top_picks.caveats: Eight-piece puzzles may need adult calm on first go — keep the four-piece ones out first. Extra play pieces are small; bag them if you still have a mouther.
top_picks.gift_note: Strong for vehicle-mad nearly-threes; compact box, under £7 direct from Orchard.
top_picks.ownership_note: Different enough from farm/animal first jigsaws to own alongside — unless you already have Orchard transport first-puzzle sets.
top_picks.safety_notes: Not suitable under 3 years — small parts on play pieces.
top_picks.review_quality_note: On-site sample small but consistently positive; Amazon listing also available under Orchard Big Wheels.
top_picks.preloved_signal_note: Check all four puzzles and six play pieces — cardboard wears well.
top_picks.evidence_notes: Amazon price varies; Orchard direct £6.50 checked 2026-07-21.

key_specs.included_items: Four farm-themed progressive jigsaws in one box
key_specs.other: SKU 1003913 — not the 18m 2–5 piece Galt first-puzzle line

top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: Four farm jigsaws in one box stepping from four pieces up to twelve. Age-marked 3+ with clear animal scenes on each board.
top_picks.ember_verdict: One box covers weeks of progression — four pieces to build confidence, twelve when they are ready to plan corners and edges. The 3+ mark is honest for nearly three; this is not the baby 18m Galt two-piece line repackaged.
top_picks.personalization_hint: likely to be asking for the next puzzle in the box after finishing an easier one
top_picks.why_it_fits: Progressive 4→12 in one purchase; honest 3+ age mark; budget-friendly; distinct from single-theme Orchard 12-piece pairs.
top_picks.caveats: Cardboard is thinner than Ravensburger — store flat. Amazon ASIN availability fluctuates; Galt direct is reliable.
top_picks.gift_note: Looks like more puzzle for the money — good unisex gift under £7.
top_picks.ownership_note: If you already own Galt farm 4-in-a-box from an older child, move to jungle/vehicles variant or Orchard theme instead.
top_picks.review_quality_note: Fewer aggregated UK reviews than Ravensburger/Orchard; strong specialist-retailer presence.
evidence_sources: Multiple UK toy retailers listing 4-6-8-12 spec
top_picks.preloved_signal_note: Very common budget puzzle set on resale sites.
top_picks.substitute_if_unavailable: Ravensburger On The Farm My First — use 4–5 piece puzzles only (longlist rank 4)
top_picks.evidence_notes: Do not confuse with Galt Ocean 2–5 piece 18m+ line (longlist rank 13).

top_picks.product_name: On The Farm — My First Jigsaw Puzzles

key_specs.other: Progressive piece count in one box

top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: Four farm mini-jigsaws in one box — two, three, four and five pieces. Extra-thick Ravensburger cardboard with bold animal pictures.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Box says 18m+ but at nearly three you want the four- and five-piece puzzles, not the two-piece warm-ups. Thick pieces survive repeated turning — good low-stakes practice before a 12-piece Orchard. Flag the age mark honestly in copy.
top_picks.personalization_hint: likely to be finishing short puzzles quickly and asking for a harder one from the same box
top_picks.why_it_fits: Excellent cardboard quality; massive Amazon review base; teaches turn-compare-try on 4–5 pieces before scaling up.
top_picks.caveats: 18m box mark fails freshness if marketed as new-stage buy — position as use the top two puzzles only. Two-piece puzzles are too easy for most 34–36m children.
top_picks.gift_note: Cheap, recognisable brand — fine as a stocking filler if recipient has no jigsaws yet.
top_picks.ownership_note: If peg puzzles and 2-piece sets are already mastered, start on the 4–5 piece puzzles or skip to rank 1.
top_picks.safety_notes: Standard small-parts guidance for youngest marked age.
top_picks.review_quality_note: Long-running UK favourite; multiple parent blogs corroborate thick-piece quality.
evidence_sources: Mum of a Premature Baby review
top_picks.preloved_signal_note: Ubiquitous on resale — pieces hold up well.
top_picks.substitute_if_unavailable: Galt 4 in a Box Farm (longlist rank 3)
top_picks.evidence_notes: Strong consensus signal across Amazon + multiple UK parent blogs for My First line.

key_specs.other: Real interlocking wooden pieces — not peg lift-out

top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: Three wooden safari jigsaws, six pieces each, in separate trays. Chunky FSC wood pieces with bold scene pictures.
top_picks.ember_verdict: For parents who want something that survives being stepped on. Six pieces is enough to require a plan, but the small trays keep the picture visible. Good counterpoint to cardboard if you already own Orchard farm sets.
top_picks.personalization_hint: likely to be naming animals and retrying the same tray until they can do it without help
top_picks.why_it_fits: Real interlocking wood at 6 pieces; UK heritage brand; budget price; distinct material from cardboard picks.
top_picks.caveats: Smaller completed size than Orchard 12-piece — less floor presence. Fewer aggregated online reviews than Amazon bestsellers.
top_picks.gift_note: Compact wooden set — nice for families avoiding plastic; under £6.
top_picks.ownership_note: Also available as Big Movers (vehicles) if safari animals are duplicated at home.
top_picks.safety_notes: Conforms to current European safety standards per manufacturer.
top_picks.review_quality_note: Specialist wooden-toy retailers stock widely; Lipscore on Bigjigs site.
top_picks.evidence_notes: Wooden inset jigsaw — verify not peg puzzle when buying other Bigjigs lines.

longlist.caveat_short: Two similar farm scenes — rotate which is out.
longlist.consensus_corroboration: Amazon UK + Orchard brand + Mumsnet Orchard puzzle trust
longlist.editorial_mentions: Mumsnet toddler toy guides (Orchard progressive jigsaws)

longlist.caveat_short: Small play pieces — bag for moutheders.
longlist.consensus_corroboration: Orchard on-site reviews + multiple UK toy shops
Galt brand + widespread UK retail listings

longlist.product_name: On The Farm — My First Jigsaw Puzzles
longlist.consensus_corroboration: Amazon UK + In The Playroom + Mum of a Premature Baby
longlist.editorial_mentions: Multiple UK parent blog reviews of Ravensburger My First

longlist.consensus_corroboration: Bigjigs brand + specialist wooden toy retailers

longlist.summary_reason: Same 2x12 format as Farm Friends — theme swap.
longlist.caveat_short: Near-duplicate of rank 1 — pick one theme.
longlist.consensus_corroboration: Amazon UK parity with Farm Friends listing

longlist.caveat_short: Very similar job to First Farm Friends.

longlist.product_name: Safari Wooden Chunky Jigsaw Puzzle in a Box (4 x 5 pieces)
longlist.summary_reason: Wooden 5-piece interlock with picture on board; safari/farm/dino variants.
longlist.caveat_short: Higher price; verify safari vs dinosaur SKU in stock.
longlist.consensus_corroboration: John Lewis category listing + Argos product support 11892

longlist.caveat_short: May feel easy for confident nearly-threes.

longlist.summary_reason: Vehicle-themed wooden 6-piece backup to Safari set.
longlist.caveat_short: Same format as Safari — choose one theme.

longlist.summary_reason: 12→24 progressive for confident puzzlers after first 12-piece success.

longlist.caveat_short: Same spec as Farm Friends — theme only difference.

longlist.product_name: 4 Puzzles in a Box — Ocean (2, 3, 4, 5 pieces)
longlist.summary_reason: Very entry-level; use only if 4–5 pieces still hard.
longlist.caveat_short: 18m mark — not a fresh 34–36m headline buy.

longlist.product_name: Jolly Dinos — My First Jigsaw Puzzles
longlist.summary_reason: Theme variant of My First 2–5 line.
longlist.caveat_short: Same age-mark issue as On The Farm My First.
longlist.editorial_mentions: Mum of a Premature Baby Jolly Dinos review

longlist.summary_reason: Wooden 6-piece dino alternative to Safari set.
longlist.caveat_short: Same format as Safari — one theme enough.

skips.product_name: Jungle Friends Jumbo Knob Puzzle (3 pieces)
skips.skip_reason: Peg/knob inset puzzle — mastered months ago at 28–30m; not real interlocking jigsaw strategy.
skips.evidence_notes: Brief explicitly excludes peg puzzles from Top Picks.

skips.skip_reason: Peg puzzle for 12m+ — bring back out if still in the toy box; not a new nearly-three buy.
Two-piece baby first puzzles — fails freshness and strategy objective for 34–36m.
50 pieces — too many for first interlocking jigsaw stage; brief caps at 12 for core picks.
skips.evidence_notes: Fine later at 3–4 years once 12-piece is easy.

skips.skip_reason: Peg puzzle marketed as jigsaw — wrong mechanism for this Stage 2 card.
Floor road builder — different play pattern (layout not single-picture strategy). Mumsnet loves it but wrong card.
skips.evidence_notes: Better as separate category if Ember adds floor-puzzle lane.

guidance_notes.text: Jigsaw sets are ideal borrow-from-a-friend or pre-loved buys — check all pieces and corner wear. Orchard and Ravensburger sell replacement pieces if one is missing.
Before buying, dig out peg puzzles and 2-piece sets from the toy box. If they are still challenging, start with Rescue Squad or Ravensburger 4–5 piece puzzles before 12-piece sets.
Hold off on 20+ piece floor puzzles and 50-piece character sets until 12-piece jigsaws feel easy — usually a few months after this band for most children.

qa_summary.notes: Ravensburger pick 4 flagged check_age_fit for 18m box mark. Bigjigs and Galt 1003913 lack large Amazon review counts — brand/specialist evidence only. Melissa & Doug chunky in-a-box backup URL points to John Lewis dinosaur SKU — verify safari variant at purchase.

required_fix_before_ingestion: Confirm Ravensburger pick 4 age-mark framing in UI copy (use 4–5 piece puzzles, not 18m headline)
Verify Melissa & Doug chunky in-a-box live UK URL if promoted from backup
ingestion_ready.founder_review_notes: Strong Orchard/Ravensburger/Galt/Bigjigs bench. One Top Pick carries check_age_fit. No peg puzzles in Top 5. Distinct best-for tags across five parent situations.
